Object.Name: Actuator
Object.Description1: Generic actuator control for valves, power switch, mower, etc.

Item.Name: default_duration_seconds
Item.Description: Duration of action started with a physical button. Setting to 0 disables the start on button press.

Item.Name: available
Item.Description: Indicate whether this actuator is available (e.g. valve connected)

Item.Name: error
Item.Description: Device and/or actuator specific error Values ------ 0: NONE 1: EXCEEDED_VALVES_LIMIT: Can't open valve because of the limit of 2. 2: VALVE_NOT_CONNECTED: Valve not connected at this time. 3: EXCEEDED_CURRENT: Valve closed because channel drains more than allowed current. 4: EXCEEDED_TOTAL_CURRENT: Valve closed because total current over max. 5: MASTER_VALVE: Master valve error. 6: DURATION_TOO_SHORT: Specified duration is too short. 7: SHORT_CIRCUIT: Valve connection is shorted. 8: EXCEEDED_SWITCH_TIMEOUT: Actuator failed to open within time limit 9: INSUFFICIENT_POWER_SUPPLY: Actuator not opened because of insufficient power supply. 10: INSUFFICIENT_TEMPERATURE: Actuator not opened because of low temperature.

Item.Name: paused_until
Item.Description: Scheduled actions paused until seconds from unix epoch

Item.Name: name
Item.Description: Name to be used for HomeKit.

Item.Name: master
Item.Description: Master channel Values ------ 0: NONE: Not linked to master channel. 1: LINKED: Linked to master channel.

Item.Name: state
Item.Description: Current actuator state Values ------ 0: INACTIVE: Actuator is currently neither ACTIVE nor performs a state transition. 1: STARTING: Actuator is transitioning to the ACTIVE state. 2: ACTIVE: Actuator is currently running but will stop in case of power loss. 3: STOPPING: Actuator is currently leaving an ACTIVE state and should soon become INACTIVE.

Item.Name: start
Item.Description: Start action of this actuator and create a new timeslot corresponding to this action. Takes two arguments: 0: integer: start source. The only purpose is to be copied over to corresponding timeslot. You can see a list of values in timeslot/initial_source. 1: integer: duration in seconds. Uses OMA format. Example usage with bnw_cli: $ bnw ipso exc "$DEVICE" actuator/0/start "as=[0='16',1='60']"

Item.Name: stop
Item.Description: Immediately stop actuator. Takes one argument: 0: integer: stop source. Same values as for start source (a.k.a. initial source). Uses OMA format. Example usage with bnw_cli: $ bnw ipso exc "$DEVICE" actuator/0/stop "as=[0='16']"
